# Telegram fetcher PoC plan implementation audit

Plan: `.github/notes/Telegram fetcher, PoC implementation plan.md`. Only the discrepancies are listed below; anything not mentioned matches the plan.

Severity: **Crit** / **High** / **Med** / **Low** / **Nit**.

## Summary

| Ref   | Gap                                                           | Sev |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | --- |
| §6.1  | [[#1. Missing `raw_hash` field on the Dialog record]]         | Low |
| §10.1 | [[#2. `connecting` lifecycle marker has no DC]]               | Low |
| §10.1 | [[#3. FLOOD_WAIT log wording differs]]                        | Nit |
| §10.1 | [[#4. Terminal summary is split into two lines]]              | Low |
| §10.1 | [[#5. `-vv` does not print TL method names per call]]         | Low |
| §11   | [[#6. Package layout differs from the plan]]                  | Low |
| §13   | [[#7. Golden-file tests for the JSONL writer are smoke-only]] | Med |

## 1. Missing `raw_hash` field on the Dialog record

Plan §6.1 lists `raw_hash: optional opaque token for resumable cursors` among the dialog record fields.

- Model: `internal/telegram/model/model.go:3-17` — no `raw_hash` field.
- Mapper: `internal/telegram/mapper/mapper.go:58-119` — field is not populated.

Effect: resumable cursoring via the raw hash is not supported. The field is declared optional, but the contract is fixed by the plan.

## 2. `connecting` lifecycle marker has no DC

Plan §10.1: `connecting to Telegram (dc=N)…` before the MTProto handshake.

- Implementation: `internal/telegram/api.go:87` prints `telegram: connecting` without `dc`. After the handshake it prints `telegram: connected` (api.go:111), also without DC.

Effect: the user does not see the DC until the handshake completes. DC is only available via `auth status`.

## 3. FLOOD_WAIT log wording differs

Plan §10.1: `FLOOD_WAIT: sleeping <duration> before retry`.

- Implementation: `internal/telegram/rate.go:61` — `telegram: flood wait` with a `duration=...` attribute. The words `sleeping` and `before retry` are absent.

Effect: cosmetic; the plan explicitly fixes this as part of the UX contract ("wording may evolve, but removing the marker counts as a regression").

## 4. Terminal summary is split into two lines

Plan §10.1: single line `done: dialogs=N messages=M flood_waits=K elapsed=…`.

- Implementation: `internal/telegram/fetch.go:112` — `done` with `dialogs=... elapsed=...`; `fetch.go:185` — a separate `done` line with `messages=... elapsed=...`.
- The `flood_waits` counter is not present in either summary line.

Effect: the user does not see the total FLOOD_WAIT count; there is no single summary line as promised by the plan.

## 5. `-vv` does not print TL method names per call

Plan §10.1 for `-vv` (debug): "Outgoing TL method names and pagination cursors per call".

- Implementation: the TL method name is logged only by the heartbeat middleware, and only when a call exceeds `--heartbeat` (`internal/telegram/api.go:132-158`). For each successful fast call there is no method name in the log.
- Retry/backoff decisions with attempt counter and chosen delay are also not printed at debug level (rate.go only logs `flood wait` at info).

Effect: at `-vv` the debug stream is thinner than the plan fixes.

## 6. Package layout differs from the plan

Plan §11 explicitly lists `internal/telegram/{mtproto, dialogs, messages, service.go}` as separate packages / files.

- Implementation is consolidated:
  - MTProto port: `internal/telegram/api.go` (no `mtproto/` package);
  - Dialogs and Messages fetchers: both inside `internal/telegram/fetch.go` (no `dialogs/`, `messages/` packages);
  - Façade: spread across `auth.go`, `resolve.go`, `fetch.go` (no `service.go`).

Effect: functionally equivalent, but the §1 rationale "Decision lock-in mitigation. All gotd types stay behind an internal port" is less well isolated — `gotd/td/tg.*` types are imported in `api.go`, `fetch.go`, and `resolve.go`, not only in `mapper/`. Swapping the library would touch more than one adapter.

## 7. Golden-file tests for the JSONL writer are smoke-only

Plan §13: "Golden-file tests — for the JSONL writer: known DTOs → exact JSONL bytes. Guards the output contract."

- Implementation: `internal/telegram/output/jsonl_test.go` only checks basic JSONL encoding (~10 lines). There are no full byte-level golden fixtures for Dialog or Message records.

Effect: the `_kind`/field schema is not pinned byte-for-byte; regressions in field order or naming will not be caught by tests.
